anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Shabda-pramana and intra-Advaita mediation, especially Rambachan, Bhamati, and Vivarana.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Substantially lowers novelty and requires splitting Advaita into self-luminosity, testimony-warranted, recognition, meditation-mediated, and undecidable strata.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If Shankara's realization depends on Upanishadic testimony, hearing, reflection, meditation, or sub-school procedures, then the self is not simply already answered in the asking in a way that bypasses all rule-like mediation. The candidate may correctly reject inference from failed objectification but overstate the absence of epistemic procedure.

claim_units: The earlier symmetric inference-policy frame misdescribes the Advaita side.
SN 22.59 can be read as a rule-like test of aggregate candidates: impermanence, stress, non-mastery, and unfitness for ownership.
Shankara's witness or consciousness is svaprakasha and self-established, not inferred as a remainder.
The comparative variable should be question-admissibility or open-question versus presupposed-answer, not merely conclusion after negation.
Predictive-processing and free-energy accounts may not neutrally adjudicate atman and anatta if they define selfhood as a model.
The practice candidate should remain a narrow test for stable adults with anxious self-verdicts, not a metaphysical teaching.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Philosophy of mind and AI self-modeling
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Debates around the Cartesian cogito, Husserlian pre-reflective self-consciousness, higher-order theories, illusionism, and AI self-models should show the same split: some accounts ask what model represents the self, while others ask whether the modeling activity already presupposes first-person givenness.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A framework can treat the subject as a candidate entity inferred from contents, or as the presupposed condition for any content and inference. If the distinction is real, it should appear wherever models try to explain the observer by modeling only observed contents.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If the distinction collapses under formalization, or if all cases can be handled by ordinary representational self-modeling without leftover presupposition disputes.

exact_claim: The symmetric reading of Advaita and early Buddhist not-self as two inference policies after the same quiet state is wrong. SN 22.59 applies a repeatable rule to the aggregates, including consciousness, then refuses ownership. Shankara's Advaita treats witnessing consciousness as self-luminous, self-established, and presupposed by any reasoning, not as a conclusion drawn from failed objectification. The decisive difference is whether the question what knows this is open at all. A predictive-processing model that defines the self as a model or posit risks pre-deciding the dispute toward not-self.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Coders find recognition, self-luminosity, presupposition, or scriptural removal of ignorance more often than a rule of the form: because consciousness cannot be objectified, therefore infer Self.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Advaita commentaries at the point of negation should avoid inferential licensing from failed objectification to Self.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of Adhyasa Bhashya, Upadesa Sahasri, BSB 2.3.7 and 2.2.28, Bhamati, Vivarana, and selected modern Advaita manuals.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Bhamati, Vivarana, BSB, or other commentaries contain explicit anumana-like licensing of the witness from failed objectification.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: SN 22.59 and related commentaries preserve a recognizable test of candidates through impermanence, stress, non-mastery, and unfitness for ownership.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The Buddhist side should show more explicit repeatable candidate-testing language in early aggregate passages.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: SN 22.59, SN 22.95, SN 44.10, Pali commentaries, Thanissaro, Bhikkhu Bodhi, and Madhyamaka pressure texts.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Commentaries treat SN 22.59 mainly as therapeutic rhetoric, not as a repeatable rule, or Madhyamaka and later Buddhist sources show the open-question frame is too narrow even for Buddhism.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Active-inference researchers either reject the category as outside the model, translate it into self-modeling or self-evidencing, or add an unmodeled presupposition that the formalism itself cannot justify.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Predictive-processing formalization cannot represent a self-disclosing, non-modeled ground without turning it into a prior, model, Markov-blanket property, or observer posit.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Expert elicitation with active-inference, predictive-processing, and philosophy-of-mind researchers, including comparison with Markov blanket realism and self-evidencing.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: A clean formalization represents non-modeled self-disclosing awareness without reducing it to a generative model, prior, blanket, or reportable content.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Dual-trained Advaita and Theravada practitioners say the crucial shift is not conclusion content but whether the question of the knower is treated as open, refused, or already presupposed.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The open-question versus presupposed-answer distinction changes practitioner counsel.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Interviews with Advaita teachers, Theravada teachers, Madhyamaka practitioners, and dual-trained contemplatives, asking for concrete student failure and repair cases.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners say this is textbook and adds no new counsel, or say teacher authority, scriptural trust, conduct, or community are more predictive than question-admissibility.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Screened stable adults with post-quiet self-verdict anxiety show reduced urge to prove presence and improved return to ordinary action without increased self-monitoring.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The Notice the Asker practice helps only a narrow stable cohort.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Two-week practice pilot excluding depersonalization or derealization disorder, OCD or scrupulosity loops, psychosis, mania, acute dissociation, severe depression, addiction withdrawal, trauma activation, and medical blackout concerns.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The practice increases derealization, self-checking, shame, reassurance seeking, or performs no better than rest, food, conversation, or ordinary grounding.

near_neighbors.difference: This candidate restates the asymmetry more sharply and adds a practice-facing line, but does not yet show a new variable beyond question-admissibility.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Decisive downgrade. Treat as a renamed consolidation unless it predicts something the prior dialogue did not.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact. The dialogue already argues that inference-policy language fits the Buddhist side better than Advaita, that Advaita treats the witness as svaprakasha and prior to pramana, and that the model must become asymmetric.
near_neighbors.source: Internal Lumenary dialogue, 2026-05-26, disclosure-exempting remainder-testing revision, reviews/dialogues/2026-05-26-whether-the-revised-taxonomy-disclosure-exempting-remainder-testing-rema.md

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate uses that Advaita account to critique an internal Lumenary comparative frame and to design a practice for anxious self-verdicts.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strong downgrade for the Advaita half. The critique is accurate but largely known.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. The source already says witnessing consciousness is unobjectifiable, self-illuminating, self-established, and prior to ordinary epistemic means, with liberation understood as recognizing what was already the case.
near_neighbors.source: SEP Shankara, self-illumination and accomplishing the accomplished, https://plato.stanford.edu/archives/sum2024/entries/shankara/

near_neighbors.difference: Fasching is philosophy of mind and Advaita exposition. The candidate adds a cross-tradition asymmetry and a critique of predictive-processing adjudication.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strong downgrade. The non-inferred self-luminosity claim is not new.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close on consciousness not being an object, not being inferred from another datum, and being directly self-revealing presence.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate admits this as anomaly, but has not integrated it as a separate testimony-warranted category.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Reduces confidence. Advaita may be neither inference nor immediate self-disclosure alone.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close and corrective. Rambachan foregrounds the Vedas or Upanishadic testimony as a source of valid knowledge in Shankara, complicating a pure self-disclosure reading.
near_neighbors.source: Anantanand Rambachan, Accomplishing the Accomplished, Google Books record, https://books.google.com/books?id=9FlsBa8FWlIC

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate calls SN 22.59 more rule-like than Advaita and uses the contrast to attack symmetry.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate downgrade. It supports the asymmetry but also weakens any claim that Buddhism simply reaches a no-self conclusion.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close on the Buddhist side. Thanissaro argues that not-self should be understood by its liberative function and that the Buddha did not answer the abstract self-exists question.

near_neighbors.difference: This candidate says more strongly that the model pre-decides toward not-self by defining the knower as a posit.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strong downgrade. The FEP claim is not new internally and may be too strong externally.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the free-energy corollary. Prior records already argue that predictive-processing or FEP frames either bias the debate or reproduce the witness/no-self gap rather than resolving it.
near_neighbors.source: Internal Lumenary, The Brain Model Already Took a Side and Formal Recurrence of the Inferential Gap

near_neighbors.difference: The paper does not claim to settle atman versus anatta. The candidate critiques the frame's hidden commitments.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction for the neuroscience bridge and a caution against metaphysical overclaim.
near_neighbors.overlap: Moderate. The paper places deconstructive meditation and nondual awareness on a predictive-processing continuum involving deconstruction of self models.
near_neighbors.source: Laukkonen and Slagter, From many to (n)one, https://researchportal.scu.edu.au/esploro/outputs/journalArticle/From-many-to-none-Meditation-and/991013062213702368

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a standalone discovery. Cross-index or merge with You Cannot Prove the One Who Is Asking, The Brain Model Already Took a Side, Stratified Post-Negation Authorization, Residual Burden of Proof After Negation, and the 2026-05-26 disclosure-exempting dialogue.
Rename the testable contribution as question-admissibility coding after negation, not The Self Was Never a Conclusion.

Build a codebook with values for open candidate test, ownership withholding, self-luminosity, presupposition, scriptural testimony, direct recognition, meditation-mediated conversion, pragmatic silence, non-positioning, neuroscience mechanism-only, analyst-frame-refused, and undecidable.
Separate early Upanishadic passage, Shankara commentary, later Advaita sub-school, early Buddhist sutta, later Buddhist commentary, and modern neuroscience explanation before comparison.
Run a blind distinct-content test against prior Lumenary records. Merge if readers cannot state the added variable without prompting.
Ask active-inference modelers to attempt non-reductive svaprakasha formalization before repeating that predictive processing pre-decides the issue.

Pilot Notice the Asker only with screened stable adults and stop for increased derealization, depersonalization, rumination, shame, compulsive checking, avoidance of feedback, panic, or clinical distress.
novelty_adjustment: Lower novelty from 0.58 to 0.29. The Advaita self-luminosity claim is well known, the Buddhist not-self strategy is well known, and the internal Lumenary dialogue from 2026-05-26 already made the decisive asymmetric correction to the inference-policy model. The FEP corollary overlaps with The Brain Model Already Took a Side and Formal Recurrence of the Inferential Gap. The human-condition fit is plausible but narrow: stable adults with anxious self-verdicts after quiet, blank, or absorbed states, especially where meaning loss and achievement-contingent self-worth are active. It should not be used for depersonalization or derealization disorder, OCD or scrupulosity checking, psychosis, mania, acute dissociation, severe depression, addiction withdrawal, trauma activation, or situations needing rest, food, human company, teacher guidance, or clinical care rather than inward attention.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Experts say the asymmetry is already standard in Advaita-Buddhist comparison, the FEP critique already exists in Lumenary or philosophy of mind, or the practice is an unsafe simplification of teacher-guided recognition.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: They identify concrete cases where question-admissibility predicts counsel, warning, or repair better than self/no-self labels, source stratum alone, or generic post-gap correction-holder models.
questions: In your tradition, is the knower recognized as already presupposed, known by scripture, inferred, refused, or left pragmatically unasked?
At the moment a student asks whether anyone was present in quiet, what concrete counsel changes if the question is treated as already answered in the asking?
Can you name a student failure case caused by treating the self as a conclusion rather than as presupposed, or by treating presupposition language as license for inflation?
Does this asymmetry add anything beyond standard Advaita self-luminosity and Buddhist not-self teaching?
Can predictive processing represent svaprakasha without reducing it to a self-model, a prior, or a Markov-blanket interpretation?
relevant_practitioners: Advaita Vedanta teachers and Sanskrit scholars
Scholars of Shankara, Bhamati, Vivarana, and Rambachan's work
Theravada meditation teachers and Pali scholars
Clinicians familiar with meditation-related depersonalization, derealization, and anxiety

primary_pair: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 3.7.23 and Shankara/self-luminosity discussions, including SEP Shankara, https://www.brhat.in/openlibrary/special/brihadaranyaka-upanishad/3-7-23 and https://plato.stanford.edu/archives/sum2024/entries/shankara/
SN 22.59 Anattalakkhana Sutta and Thanissaro, The Not-Self Strategy, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/SN/SN22_59.html and https://www.buddhistinquiry.org/article/the-not-self-strategy/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: Very high. The idea risks replacing one simplification with another. Shankara's non-inferred witness does not mean no method, no scripture, no teacher, or no pramana function. Thanissaro's not-self strategy does not exhaust Buddhist anatta, and Madhyamaka may refuse both self and no-self framing. Predictive processing does not have one settled ontology of self-models, Markov blankets, or self-evidencing. The practice version may worsen derealization, depersonalization, OCD or scrupulosity, rumination, mania, psychosis, severe depression, trauma activation, addiction withdrawal, or avoidance of ordinary feedback.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Before raising novelty, read Shankara's Adhyasa Bhashya, Upadesa Sahasri, Brahma Sutra Bhashya 2.3.7 and 2.2.28, Bhamati, Vivarana, Sureśvara, Mayeda, Rambachan, Comans, Suthren Hirst, Bina Gupta, Ram-Prasad, Timalsina, MacKenzie, Fasching, Albahari, Bhikkhu Bodhi's critique of purely strategic anatta readings, Madhyamaka non-positioning, Laukkonen and Slagter, Bruineberg on Markov blankets, Friston on self-evidencing, Metzinger, Letheby, and internal Lumenary records: Residual Burden of Proof After Negation, the 2026-05-26 disclosure-exempting dialogue, The Brain Model Already Took a Side, Formal Recurrence of the Inferential Gap, You Cannot Prove the One Who Is Asking, and Stratified Post-Negation Authorization.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 names the unseen seer and says no other seer, hearer, perceiver, or knower stands beyond it. Shankara and later Advaita discussions treat witnessing consciousness as unobjectifiable, self-illuminating, and prior to ordinary pramanas. SN 22.59, by contrast, asks of each aggregate whether it is constant, easeful, subject to mastery, and fit to regard as mine, self, or what I am, then applies this to every consciousness in the aggregate range. This supports an asymmetric comparison: one side can be read as recognition or presupposition of the knower, the other as disciplined refusal of ownership. But the candidate overstates if it says Advaita runs no rule at all, because scripture, commentary, hearing, reflection, meditation, and sub-school debates still mediate realization.

title: The Self Was Never a Conclusion
unlike_statement: Unlike Advaita self-luminosity scholarship, Thanissaro's not-self strategy, and prior Lumenary disclosure-exempting dialogue, this candidate says the master comparative error is question-admissibility: one side treats what knows this as already present in the asking, while the other disciplines the urge to own any candidate. That sharper phrasing is useful, but mostly renames an already recorded correction.
